name: ROBERT O. HUTCHESON
motto: "Night after night, He sat, and bleared his eyes with books."

name: CHARLES JOEL
motto: "Silent and soft and slow."

name: JOSIAH TATTNALL KOLLOCK
motto: "Come hither from the furrow, and be merry."

name: ROBERT A. LAIRD,
motto: "I am not in the roll of common men."

name: H. STANLEY LANGSTON,
motto: "O, nightingale, Cease from thy enamoured tale."

name: ROY E. LANHAM,
motto: "Lofty and sour to them that loved him not; But, to those men that sought him, sweet as summer."

name: WALTER M. LUCAS
motto: "On their own merits modest men are dumb."

name: CHARLES M. McCLEAN
motto: "Sang in tones of deep emotion, Songs of love and songs of longing."

name: WALTER AZMER MANN
motto: "What means this passionate discourse, This devotion with such circumstance?"

name: CHARLES E. MARTIN
motto: "I'll make me glorious by my pen, And famous by my sword."

name: WILLIAM KING MEADOW
motto: "Others may praise what they like; But I, from the banks of the running Missouri, praise nothing."

name: HAROLD D. MEYER
motto: "Sir, your wit ambles well; it goes easily."

name: BERT MICHAEL
motto: "A man he seemed of cheerful yesterdays And confident tomorrows."

name: THOMAS I. MILLER
motto: "In each cheek appears a pretty dimple."

name: WILLIAM HAMP MULLINS
motto: "Studious of ease and fond of humble things."

name: CHARLES H. NEWSOM,
motto: "The applause! delight! the wonder of our age! May Shakespere rise."

name: WILLIAM PERRIN NICOLSON, Jr.,
motto: "So faithful in love, and so dauntless in war, There was never a knight like Young Lochinvar."

name: GEORGE TRAYLOR NORTHEN,
motto: "Importune him for moneys; be not eas'd With slight denial; nor then silenced."

name: MARVIN BANKS PERRY
motto: "O gentle Romeo, If thou dost love, pronounce it faithfully."

name: JOHN ROBERT RADFORD, JR.
motto: "Discretion of speech is more than eloquence."

name: WILLIAM ARTHUR REID
motto: "He wrote for divers papers, which, as everybody knows, Is worse than serving in a shop or scaring off the crows."

name: JOSEPH H. ROSS,
motto: "Whence is thy learning? Hath thy toil O'er books consumed the midnight oil?"

name: HENRY D. RUSSELL,
motto: "Old politicians shew on wisdom past; And totters on in business to the last."

name: RICHARD BOWIE SAXON,
motto: "'Tis not for Mortals always to be blest."

name: AUGUSTUS O. BACON SPARKS
motto: "Would not we shatter it to bits,—and then Remould it nearer to the Heart's desire?"

name: CHARLES H. STONE
motto: "Too proud and too sensitive to be happy."

name: BENJAMIN F. STOVALL
motto: "Such men as he be never at heart's ease."

name: E. R. STUMP
motto: "Art for Art's sake."

name: JOHN RALPH TIBBETS
motto: "Many men resemble glass, smooth, polished and dull so long as unbroken—then sharp; every splinter pricks."

name: SCOTT H. TITSHAW
motto: "And some loquacious vessels were."

name: BENTON H. WALTON,
motto: "In a luckless moment he discovered men Rise to high position thru a ready pen."

name: JAMES HAMILTON WARE,
motto: "Knowledge is the knowing that we can not know."

name: ANDREW L. WEST,
motto: "Since Knowledge is but Sorrow's spy, It is not safe to know."

name: ROBERT FRED WHELCHEL
motto: "God bless you, I have nothing to tell, sir."

name: THOMAS JACKSON WOOFTER, Jr.
motto: "Beside, he was a shrewd Philosopher, And had read every text and gloss."

name: KENYON B. ZAHNER
motto: "He would not, with a peremptory tone Assert the nose upon his face his own."

name: Fitzpatrick, Mark William
motto: ,name:

name: Stephens, R. P.
description: Dr. R. P. Stephens. Member duly qualified.

name: Salyer, S. M.
description: Prof. S. M. Salyer. Member duly qualified.

description: Dr. H. A. Nix. Member duly qualified. Telegram Specialty.

description: Member duly qualified.

description: Member duly qualified.

description: Member duly qualified.

description: Member duly qualified.

description: Member duly qualified.

name: Durden
description: Member duly qualified.

name: Maddox, A. K.
description: Member duly qualified.

name: Payne, W. Oscar
description: Prof. W. Oscar Payne. Candidate for membership.

name: Estes, Chas. T.
description: Candidate for membership.

name: Phillips, Frank
description: Candidate for membership.

name: Malone
description: Candidate for membership.

name: Turk, William Telford
description: Ruled out for flirting.

name: Northen, George
description: Ruled out for flirting.

name: Newsome
description: Ruled out for flirting.

name: Yampolsky
description: Ruled out for flirting.

name: Wood, Jared Irwin
motto: ,name:

name: JOHN Z. RYAN
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Senior Law Class Officers

name: WILLIAM SPENCER CONNERAT
motto: "Among them, but not of them."

name: ROSS ADDISON CREEKMORE
motto: "He could songs make, and well."

name: CARL FOSTER CROSSLEY
motto: "Full of wise saws and modern instances."

name: LOVICK GEORGE FORTSON
motto: "Zeal moved thee; To please thy gods thou didst it."

name: WIMBERLY POWELL McLEOD
motto: "Then he will talk—good gods, how he will talk."

name: JOHN MILTON MORROW
motto: "And thou art long, and lank, and brown, As is the ribbed sea-sand."

name: John Thomas Murray
motto: "Soft blows the wind that breathes from that blue sky."

name: Henry Alexander Newman
motto: "Long it is since I saw him, But time hath nothing blur'd those lines of favor Which he wore."

name: Frank Victor Paradise
motto: "If she undervalue me, What care I how fair she be?"

name: FRANK THOMAS PHILLIPS,
motto: "The sweetest hours that I ever spend Are spent among the lassies, Oh."

name: THOMAS NIMMONS POWELL,
motto: "Bold of your worthiness, we single you As our best moving fair solicitor."

name: JOHN ZEIGLER RYAN,
motto: "He sang the bold anthem of Erin go braugh."

name: EUGENE HILLIARD SPALDING
motto: "Be not the first by whom the new are tried, Nor yet the last to lay the old aside."

name: REUBEN McDANIEL TUCK
motto: "Love all, trust a few, Do wrong to none."

name: JOSEPH EUGENE VARELA
motto: "I celebrate myself, and sing myself, And what I assume you shall assume."

name: Augustus Octavius Bacon Sparks
description: Charter Member. (Owing to the fact that every member ran for office and that every one voted for himself, the election is still in doubt.)

description: Charter Member. (Owing to the fact that every member ran for office and that every one voted for himself, the election is still in doubt.)

name: C. H. Stone
description: Author of the poem 'Wake! Wake! Wake! (The Annual Parody)'.

name: A. K. Maddox
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Junior Law Class Officers

name: Lynch, James Montgomery
description: Author of "Why I Shall Not Sing Next Season for the Metropolitan Opera Company." In this treatise, the author answers a question which many of his friends and a host of newspaper representatives have propounded.

name: Russell
description: Co-author of "The Theory and Practice of Politics." In this compendium, Messrs. Russell, Brown, Sparks, and Deadwyler unite their efforts in reducing an abtruse and vague subject to clear-cut scientific principles. Coming from such a source, the book should carry weight.

name: Brown
description: Co-author of "The Theory and Practice of Politics." In this compendium, Messrs. Russell, Brown, Sparks, and Deadwyler unite their efforts in reducing an abtruse and vague subject to clear-cut scientific principles. Coming from such a source, the book should carry weight.

name: Sparks
description: Co-author of "The Theory and Practice of Politics." In this compendium, Messrs. Russell, Brown, Sparks, and Deadwyler unite their efforts in reducing an abtruse and vague subject to clear-cut scientific principles. Coming from such a source, the book should carry weight.

name: Deadwyler
description: Co-author of "The Theory and Practice of Politics." In this compendium, Messrs. Russell, Brown, Sparks, and Deadwyler unite their efforts in reducing an abtruse and vague subject to clear-cut scientific principles. Coming from such a source, the book should carry weight.

name: Johnson, James A.
description: Author of "Money." Guaranteed to give you a handsome Fifth Avenue residence in a month. Read fully his history and his tutors of this line.

name: Koplin, Louis
description: Composer of music for "I Yampolskylighti," a Grand Opera in Five Acts. In criticising this elaborate composition, Omar Weems, the distinguished musical authority, said: "The work is almost as good as if I had done it myself—I fear, however, that we shall not hear it often."

name: Stone, Charles H.
description: Author of the libretto for "I Yampolskylighti," a Grand Opera in Five Acts. In criticising this elaborate composition, Omar Weems, the distinguished musical authority, said: "The work is almost as good as if I had done it myself—I fear, however, that we shall not hear it often."

name: Weems, Omar
description: A distinguished musical authority who, in criticising the composition "I Yampolskylighti," said: "The work is almost as good as if I had done it myself—I fear, however, that we shall not hear it often."

name: Sparks, Augustus Octavius
description: Author of "The Fashion Book." To the man who swears his love to style, drink deep of this cup and be a sport. An inspiration of the fair sex.

name: Creekmore, Ross
description: Author of "Athletics at Georgia." In this publication, the author explains how to make every team in college. His superb physique is his main advertisement, and his experience is unlimited. Read carefully and thereby make a "G" in two weeks.

name: Farmer, R. S.
description: Author of "Military Promotions." An excellent thesis on the value of boot-licking. Every soldier or would-be soldier, who aspires to military glory, should read this book.

name: Burrage
description: Author of "My Method of Beauty." To appreciate this book one should see the author. Would that his picture took the place of these words! Venus would fade into insignificance and the two-by-four drug stores would go out of business. No doubt this book will have a wide circulation.

name: Titshaw, Scott H.
description: Author of "Fifteen Undecisive Battles of the World." The author here recounts the details of all the scraps, class against class, and boy against boy, that he has witnessed during his sojourn here. The narration is vivid and shows a surprising accuracy of detail.

name: NELSON S. ARTHUR
motto: "When I was sick you gave bitter pills."

name: THOMAS CLARK, JR.
motto: "Half our knowledge we must snatch, not take."

name: TURNER FRANK COILE
motto: "And show me samples of a thousand names, Telling their strange and vigorous faculties."

name: Harper, William Carey
motto: "Now let it work: mischief, thou art afoot, Take thou what course thou wilt."

name: Parkerson, Gus Monroe
motto: "Methinks you prescribe to yourself very preposterously."

name: Swift, Albert Manning
motto: "Thou hast no figures, nor no fantasies, Which busy care draws in the brains of men."

name: JOHN BINION WOODCOCK
motto: "Throw physic to the dogs; I'll none of it."

name: E. I. Ransom
description: O Chemistry! Thou art the bane of life, To catch the unsuspecting and to cost Poor Juniors dips in thine unequal strife. O Homer! How couldst thou be so Black, to vent On unsuspecting men thy wicked wit, When none have strength or courage to prevent Thy cutting satire that causes laughter loud. Be White, O Black, and give us all a rise, We've worked like slaves to learn thy cursed stuff; We've seen thee laugh when some poor Junior died, Then shoot another; consign me then to Wells Whose cooling depths of H2O will hide My biting grief. Ring out, O tolling bells, For shot I am; my dip is lost. Good-bye.
